name: Signature Bank
description_short: Signature Bank is a Chicago-based community bank offering commercial and personal banking services with FDIC insurance, combining big-bank capabilities with personalized relationship-driven service.
description_long:
Signature Bank is a Chicago-headquartered financial institution that positions itself as a relationship-driven community bank serving the Midwest. The bank was established with a focus on personalized service in the commercial and personal banking sectors, and recently became part of Esquire Financial Holdings, Inc., which acquired Signature Bancorporation Inc. to expand into the Chicago banking market.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of services across three main divisions: commercial banking (including commercial lending, treasury management, corporate cards, and fraud protection), personal banking (checking, savings, branch access, and Zelle money transfer), and wealth management (investment advice, trust administration, and estate planning support). All deposit products are FDIC-insured, providing federal protection up to applicable limits. Their digital banking platform provides online and mobile access, and they maintain physical branch locations throughout Chicago and the Midwest.

Signature Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on relationship-based banking rather than transactional interactions, emphasizing that customers "always get to talk with a real person." The bank highlights being "capitalized well beyond the regulatory recommendation" to ensure financial stability, and showcases customer success stories demonstrating their work with multi-generational family businesses and commercial real estate developers. They position themselves as providing "the best personalized business banking experience available in the Midwest" with community involvement and commitment to Chicago neighborhoods.

As a regional community bank, Signature Bank serves small-to-mid-market commercial clients and personal banking customers in the Chicago area. While the website demonstrates financial strength and relationship-focused service philosophy, the actual product offerings and fee structures are not detailed on their public website. Like all regional banks, they may have geographic limitations and more limited digital offerings compared to national or neobank competitors. Prospective customers should request specific information about account minimums, fee schedules, and loan terms before opening accounts. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Commercial lending and customized financing solutions
Business fraud protection and risk management
Trust services and estate planning support
Branch locations throughout Chicago and Midwest
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Personalized relationship banking model emphasizing direct access to real banking professionals
Capitalized well beyond regulatory requirements, demonstrating financial strength and stability
Comprehensive commercial lending solutions including commercial real estate financing expertise
Wealth management and trust services with professional fiduciary management
Digital banking platform with online and mobile access plus physical branch locations
Community-focused with documented commitment to Chicago neighborhoods and local business growth
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted primarily to Chicago and Midwest region
No pricing, fee, or account minimum information disclosed on website
No details on loan terms, APRs, or specific product rates provided
Smaller institution may lack some technology features and services of national banks
Website lacks information on mobile app capabilities or digital banking features

contact.address: 9450 Bryn Mawr Ave Ste 300, Rosemont, IL 60018

best_for: Chicago-area small to mid-market business owners seeking relationship-driven commercial banking
High-net-worth individuals and families in the Midwest seeking personalized wealth management
Commercial real estate developers and investors in Chicago seeking specialized CRE financing
Personal banking customers in Illinois and surrounding states valuing local community connection
diagnosis: Signature Bank is best for Chicago-area businesses and personal customers who prioritize relationship-based banking and personalized service over large-scale digital convenience. The main caveat is that as a regional community bank, they serve a limited geographic footprint and may not match the technology depth or product variety of larger national institutions, requiring direct contact for specific pricing and terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 9450 Bryn Mawr Ave Ste 300, Rosemont, IL 60018
